v1_bug.description: SEC M7 date correction candidate: v1 first rescheduled the meeting to 13/05/2026 10:00, then the user corrected it to 14/05/2026 10:00 about one minute later.
SECI P22.1 reassignment round-trip candidate: v1 reassigned from Rodrigo Lima to Mariany, then the same user corrected it back to Rodrigo Lima about two minutes later.
v1_bug.expected_correction: Human review required; v2 should not be judged compliant solely for repeating the first reassignment if the intended assignee was Rodrigo Lima.

v1_bug.description: Thiago P6/T56 deadline correction candidate: v1 applied the 24/04 deadline to P6, then the user clarified that the deadline belonged to T56.
v1_bug.expected_correction: Deadline 2026-04-24 should apply to T56, not P6.

state_drift.description: The synced DB already reflects the T51 reassignment and co-responsible note before the confirmation turn, so v2 confirms current state without a target-turn mutation.
state_drift.evidence: taskflow snapshot: T51 assigned to Mário with Flávia co-responsible note already present
state_drift.expected_with_historical_snapshot: Historical pre-confirmation state would let the bare 'Sim' perform the mutation on T51.

state_drift.description: Generated corpus final_response is misaligned with the prior SEC-T41 user message; this turn's user text asks to update T44. The synced DB is post-event and T44 is already done, so v2's current-state answer is a state-drift/no-op result.
state_drift.expected_with_historical_snapshot: Historical pre-turn state would move or annotate T44 as waiting for documentation.

v1_bug.description: Generated corpus has no v1 outbound for an explicit SEC-T41 note update, but v2 correctly records the note through MCP. Treat v2's visible confirmation as a human-review improvement over v1 silence.
v1_bug.expected_correction: v2 should preserve the SEC-T41 note mutation instead of reproducing the no-response behavior.

state_drift.description: The synced DB already has T11 in done, so v2 returns a current-state failure/confirmation instead of replaying the historical completion.
state_drift.expected_with_historical_snapshot: Historical pre-turn state would allow the completion or no-op silently, depending on the exact v1 session state.

v1_bug.description: SECI exact-ID lookup 'p11' has no v1 outbound in the generated corpus. Exact task IDs should be read, not ignored.
v1_bug.expected_correction: v2 should return P11 details instead of reproducing v1 silence.

v1_bug.description: SECI standalone multi-step activity has no v1 outbound in the generated corpus. Silent no-op is not useful for cutover.
v1_bug.expected_correction: v2 should ask how to capture or attach the activity instead of timing out or silently ignoring it.

state_drift.description: Generated corpus final_response includes neighboring SEC-T41/T44 replies from delayed v1 batching, but this user turn asks only to note and reassign SEC T96. The synced DB already contains the T96 note and Maura assignment, so an idempotent no-op confirmation is acceptable.
state_drift.expected_with_historical_snapshot: Historical pre-turn state would add the T96 note and reassign T96 to Maura on board-sec-taskflow.

state_drift.description: Generated corpus final_response includes the neighboring T56 confirmation, but this user turn asks only for the data-center/sala task. Judge the task creation as T57 vs the replay allocator's current next ID.
state_drift.expected_with_historical_snapshot: Create the data-center/sala task; historical v1 allocated T57.

v1_bug.description: Generated production corpus has no v1 outbound for the dependent e-Governe task turn even though the neighboring history shows the task was created/configured. Treat v2's confirmation/read as a human-review improvement over v1 silence, not as a migration regression.
v1_bug.expected_correction: v2 may confirm the existing T56/T54 dependency state instead of reproducing v1's no-response behavior.

v1_bug.description: SECI detailed project/activity/notes report request has no v1 observable reply in the generated corpus, but the user explicitly asked for a report. Silent no-op should not be mirrored.
v1_bug.expected_correction: v2 should answer with a detailed project report using first-class TaskFlow reads.

v1_bug.description: SECI image-only message has no v1 observable reply in the generated corpus. A silent no-op is not useful; without image understanding/context, v2 should ask for clarification rather than inventing a TaskFlow mutation.
v1_bug.expected_correction: v2 should ask for context or explain that it cannot infer the requested TaskFlow action from the image alone.

v1_bug.description: SECI bare reassignment 'Atribuir para Rodrigo' has no task ID and no reply_to context in messages.db. The immediately prior visible context was P11.11, but v1 reassigned P22.1. Do not mirror that unsafe task inference.
v1_bug.expected_correction: v2 should ask which task to assign instead of guessing P22.1.

state_drift.description: Historical v1 created P2.15 on April 30, but the synced May 16 DB snapshot already contains P2.15. v2 correctly allocates the next free P2 child ID instead of overwriting/reusing an occupied historical ID.
state_drift.evidence: Phase 3 replay on synced snapshot created P2.16 and confirmed it in outbound text.
state_drift.expected_with_historical_snapshot: With an exact pre-April-30 snapshot, v2 should allocate P2.15; with the synced May 16 snapshot, P2.16 is the safe corrected path.

state_drift.description: SETD STRANS/Gov.BR note is already present on parent SEC-T56 in the synced production snapshot, so v2 should find T56 and confirm current state instead of creating a duplicate inbox task.
state_drift.evidence: taskflow snapshot: board-sec-taskflow T56 notes include 'Aguardando implementação de ambiente de homologação do Gov.BR por parte da Truetec.'
state_drift.expected_with_historical_snapshot: Historical pre-turn state would add the note to T56; synced state should produce an idempotent confirmation.

v1_bug.description: SETD role-directory question made v1 ask for Wendel's role instead of answering the explicit directory question. Do not mirror the deflection.
v1_bug.expected_correction: v2 should answer cargos/papéis from first-class Taskflow directory data.

v1_bug.description: SETD 'E Laizys?' is an org-directory follow-up. v1 anchored it to a missing meeting-creation context; fresh compliance should answer where Laizys is registered instead of inventing that context.
v1_bug.expected_correction: v2 should read the Taskflow org directory/person lookup and answer Laizys's boards.

v1_bug.description: SETD sector placement command is explicit: put Edilson on SM-SETD-SECTI and Hudson on PO-SETD-SECTI. v1 failed to resolve the existing contacts/boards cleanly; do not preserve that failure.
v1_bug.expected_correction: v2 should reuse the existing people and mutate board_people through api_add_board_person/api_remove_board_person.
